title: Boat Rental in Mauritius: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Mauritius need not break the bank. From Grand Baie, a half-day small motorboat starts at around 8,000 MUR, while skippered charters in Mauritius offer shared tours from 3,500 MUR per person via BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Mauritius?

A shared tour costs from 3,500 MUR per person, while a private half-day motorboat rental starts at 8,000 MUR. The price depends on boat type, duration, and season. A full-day catamaran for up to 12 guests typically costs 35,000, 55,000 MUR including lunch. Crewed yachts with skipper are more, starting at 70,000 MUR per day. For the latest listings, compare verified options on skippered charters in Mauritius.

Where to set off in Mauritius

Most boat rentals depart from the north or west coast. Key marinas and bays include:

  • Grand Baie, the main hub, with the most boats for hire and easy access to Coin de Mire and Flat Island.
  • Pointe aux Canonniers, a quieter launch point just south of Grand Baie.
  • Flic en Flac, a popular west‑coast departure for the marine park of Île aux Bénitiers.
  • Black River (Rivière Noire), the gateway to the remote Île aux Cerfs and the Morne Brabant area.
  • Mahébourg, on the south‑east coast, ideal for exploring Blue Bay Marine Park.
  • Rivière des Galets, known for its protected launch area near the Tamarin estuary.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (5, 6 m), perfect for a day trip to beach coves, up to 4 guests, from 8,000 MUR half‑day.
  2. Dinghy with outboard, the cheapest option to zip around the lagoons, up to 3 guests, from 4,000 MUR half‑day.
  3. Catamaran (12, 15 m), spacious for groups, up to 12 guests, from 35,000 MUR full‑day including meal.
  4. Motor yacht (Princess V58), luxurious 58‑foot yacht with flybridge, up to 10 guests; see the Princess V58 charter page for rates.
  5. Sailing catamaran (Lagoon 450), a stable cruising cat, up to 8 guests, from 50,000 MUR full‑day with skipper.

Price breakdown

  • Shared tour: 3,500, 5,000 MUR per person, best for solo travellers.
  • Small motorboat half‑day: 8,000, 14,000 MUR, ideal for 2, 4 people.
  • Catamaran full‑day: 35,000, 55,000 MUR, covering 6, 12 guests with lunch.
  • Luxury crewed yacht full‑day: 70,000, 120,000 MUR, includes skipper and host.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Mauritius?

For boats under 5 horsepower, no licence is required; for larger boats a local licence or an international certificate may be needed. Visitors can hire a skipper, which avoids licence paperwork. Your operator will confirm the exact requirements. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Mauritius or list your own.

For a quieter experience, launch from Black River in the morning. The sea breeze picks up around 10 a.m. making the sail to Île aux Cerfs smooth.

Season at a glance

The high season runs from May to November, when the south‑east trade winds (the Alizés) stay steady, making sailing conditions ideal. December to April is the humid summer with occasional cyclones, so many boats are less active.

At a glance

  • Price band: 3,500, 120,000 MUR per day
  • Peak months: May to November
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half‑day) to 8 hours (full‑day)
  • Max capacity: 12 guests on a catamaran
  • Currency: Mauritian rupee (MUR)

Questions about boat rental in Mauritius

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Only very small boats (under 5 hp) may be rented bareboat; for larger vessels you typically need a local licence or must hire a skipper.

What is the best month for boat hire?

October is ideal, with calm seas and mild winds in the 10, 15 knot range along the west coast.

How far is Île aux Cerfs from the mainland?

It is about 5 nautical miles from the Black River jetty, a 30‑minute boat ride at cruising speed.

Whether you are planning a day on the water or a longer voyage, BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare boats in Mauritius. If you own a vessel, you can become a host and earn extra income. In 2026, the island continues to offer affordable charters for every budget.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Mauritius and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
